title: Gas Mileage & Fuel Efficiency Calculator
whatIsIt: The Gas Mileage Calculator is a performance monitoring tool that determines your vehicle's actual fuel efficiency (MPG or L/100km). While manufacturers provide estimated ratings, real-world mileage fluctuates based on driving habits, vehicle load, and engine health. This tool allows drivers to track performance over time and detect potential mechanical issues through sudden efficiency drops.
formula:
To calculate mileage, you use the 'Fill-to-Fill' method: 

$$	ext{MPG} =  rac{	ext{Miles Driven}}{	ext{Gallons Consumed}}$$

For metric systems: 

$$	ext{L/100km} = \left(  rac{	ext{Liters Consumed}}{	ext{Kilometers Driven}} ight) 	imes 100$$
example:
If your odometer read 15,000 miles at your last fill-up and 15,300 miles today, and you pumped exactly 10 gallons: 
1. Distance: $15,300 - 15,000 = 300$ miles. 
2. Efficiency: $300 / 10 = 30$ MPG. 

This indicates your car is performing within standard efficiency for a compact or mid-sized sedan.
useCases: Vehicle Health Monitoring: Identifying engine or tire pressure issues through a sudden decrease in fuel economy.
Cost Tracking: Budgeting for monthly fuel costs based on proven real-world efficiency rather than estimates.
Upgrade Justification: Determing if switching to a hybrid or electric vehicle will provide significant long-term savings.
Eco-Driving: Measuring the impact of smoother acceleration and reduced idling on your car's range.

faqs.question: Why is my real mileage lower than the EPA sticker?
faqs.answer: EPA estimates are conducted in controlled laboratory settings. Real-world factors like wind resistance, cold weather, heavy traffic, and AC usage significantly increase fuel consumption.

faqs.question: Does tire pressure affect gas mileage?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Under-inflated tires increase rolling resistance. For every 1 PSI drop in all four tires, you can lose roughly 0.2% of your fuel economy.

faqs.question: Is it and MPG or Kilometers per Liter?
faqs.answer: The US and UK primarily use MPG. Most of Europe and Canada use L/100km, which measures how much fuel is needed to reach a fixed distance (the inverse of MPG).

deepDive: Gas mileage is more than just a number on a dashboard; it's a measure of thermal efficiency. Internal combustion engines typically only convert 20-30% of their fuel's energy into movement. The rest is lost as heat. High-mileage vehicles improve this ratio through direct injection, turbocharging, and weight reduction. By tracking your MPG, you are essentially monitoring the energy-conversion efficiency of your engine.

glossary.definition: The instrument on a vehicle that records the total distance traveled.
A measure of the fuel efficiency of any internal combustion engine that burns fuel and produces shaft power.
The force resisting the motion when a body (tire) rolls on a surface.
